summary: The Docker-based reproduction builds Traefik v1.7.25 from source, starts it with the dashboard enabled on port 8080, and sends a GET / request with X-Forwarded-Prefix: ////evil.com. The server responds with a 302 redirect to //evil.com/dashboard/, confirming the open redirect vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The server should return HTTP 302 with a Location header containing //evil.com/dashboard/, which is a protocol-relative URL that browsers resolve to the attacker-controlled domain evil.com.
actual_observed_result: The server responded with HTTP 302 and Location: //evil.com/dashboard/, confirming that the safePrefix validator was bypassed and the attacker-controlled prefix was embedded in the redirect target.
verification_method: The verify script captures the HTTP response headers, checks that the status code is 302 and that the Location header contains the string //evil.com. This distinguishes the open redirect from a normal redirect to /dashboard/ and from unrelated errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP response status 302 confirming the redirect was triggered.
Location header containing //evil.com/dashboard/ confirming the attacker-controlled prefix was embedded.
Traefik startup log confirming the dashboard was enabled and serving on the traefik entrypoint.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
